mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations of RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing limits, prompting inflation expectations that force central banks to tighten policy and generate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields positive 20-day forward returns during late-cycle expansions, or the signal predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 5.0% creates a high discount rate environment that suppresses equity valuations and triggers immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k and UNRATE declines > 0.1pp simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to generate a positive Rank IC above 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and equity cash flow expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals supply chain disruptions that reduce near-term earnings visibility and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following a 0.5% monthly drop in INDPRO during recovery phases.
